---
title: "Emissions Data - Table, Map, and Chart"
format: html
echo: false

---


```{python}
import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px
from itables import show
```


```{python}
# Load the data
emissions = pd.read_csv("data/co2_cons.csv")

```

```{python}
# Subset the data to include `name`, `2000`, and `2022` columns only.
table_df = emissions[["name", "2000", "2022"]].copy()
```

```{python}

# Calculate absolute change as the difference between 2022 and 2000
table_df["Absolute Change"] = table_df["2022"] - table_df["2000"]
```

```{python}


# Calculate relative change as the absolute change divided by the 2000 emissions, then multiplied by 100
table_df["Relative Change"] = (table_df["Absolute Change"] / table_df["2000"]) * 100
```

```{python}

# Round to 0 decimal places, and add a % sign to the relative change
table_df["Relative Change"] = table_df["Relative Change"].round(0).astype(str) + "%"
```


```{python}
# Melt the original `emissions` dataset. Your id_vars should be
# ["geo", "name"], your var_name should be "year", and your
# value_name should be "emissions".
emissions_long = emissions.melt(id_vars=["geo", "name"], var_name="year", value_name="emissions")
```

```{python}

# Convert year to numeric using pd.to_numeric
emissions_long["year"] = pd.to_numeric(emissions_long["year"])
```

```{python}

# Convert emissions to numeric using pd.to_numeric. Here, we also convert dashes to the minus sign
emissions_long["emissions"] = pd.to_numeric(
    emissions_long["emissions"].astype(str).str.replace("−", "-")
)
```

```{python}


# Query for years between 1990 and 2022 (that is 1990, 1991, ..., 2021, 2022)
emissions_long_1990_2022 = emissions_long.query("year >= 1990 and year <= 2022")

```

```{python}

# Query for 5 countries using the `name` column (adjust these to any countries you like)
emissions_long_subset = emissions_long_1990_2022.query("name in ['Bahrain', 'China', 'India', 'Germany', 'Brazil']")

```

```{python}

# Create line chart. Year should be on the x-axis, emissions on the y-axis, and color should be by name.
fig_chart = px.line(
    emissions_long_subset,
    x="year",
    y="emissions",
    color="name",
    title="CO2 Emissions (1990-2022) for Selected Countries",
)

```


```{python}
# Convert the existing ISO3 codes to uppercase
emissions_long_1990_2022["country_code"] = emissions_long_1990_2022["geo"].str.upper()

fig_map = px.choropleth(
    emissions_long_1990_2022,
    locations="country_code",
    color="emissions",
    hover_name="name",
    animation_frame="year",
    title="Global CO2 Emissions (1990-2022)",
)

```


# Consumption-based CO₂ emissions

Annual consumption-based CO₂ emissions (tonnes)

::: {.panel-tabset}

## Table

```{python}
show(table_df)
```

## Chart

```{python}
fig_chart.show()
```

## Map

```{python}
fig_map.show()
```

:::
